{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Corporate Accountant",
  "description": "Iraizickify Business Store is a reliable ICT and business support service provider specializing in company and business registration under the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) in Nigeria. We are committed to helping entrepreneurs, startups, and established businesses formalize and grow their ventures with ease and professionalism.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Corporate Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Gather financial data and ledgers.
	Consolidate and analyze financial statements and results.
	Prepare budgets and monitor expenditures.
	Handle monthly, quarterly and annual closings.
	Manage periodical reporting.
	Oversee external and internal audits.
	Analyze finances to determine risks and create forecasts.
	Advise management on how to craft effective business plans and resolve cost-related issues.
	Supervise&nbsp;Junior Accountants.


Requirements and Skills


	BSc / BA in Accounting, Finance or related field; professional certification (e.g. CPA) is a plus.
	Proven experience as a Corporate Accountant.
	Excellent knowledge of accounting regulations and practices.
	In-depth experience in risk analysis, budgeting and forecasting.
	Proficient in MS Office (especially Excel) and finance software.
	An analytical mind with problem-solving aptitude.
	Excellent communication skills.
	Keen eye for detail.
	Organizational and leadership skills.


Salary
N120,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and&nbsp;qualified&nbsp;should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr.iraizickifyltd@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Corporate Accountant Job at Iraizickify Business Store




        
    

        
        Iraizickify Business Store is a reliable ICT and business support service provider specializing in company and business registration under the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) in Nigeria. We are committed to helping entrepreneurs, startups, and established businesses formalize and grow their ventures with ease and professionalism.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Corporate Accountant

Job Location: Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Gather financial data and ledgers.
	Consolidate and analyze financial statements and results.
	Prepare budgets and monitor expenditures.
	Handle monthly, quarterly and annual closings.
	Manage periodical reporting.
	Oversee external and internal audits.
	Analyze finances to determine risks and create forecasts.
	Advise management on how to craft effective business plans and resolve cost-related issues.
	Supervise Junior Accountants.


Requirements and Skills


	BSc / BA in Accounting, Finance or related field; professional certification (e.g. CPA) is a plus.
	Proven experience as a Corporate Accountant.
	Excellent knowledge of accounting regulations and practices.
	In-depth experience in risk analysis, budgeting and forecasting.
	Proficient in MS Office (especially Excel) and finance software.
	An analytical mind with problem-solving aptitude.
	Excellent communication skills.
	Keen eye for detail.
	Organizational and leadership skills.


Salary
N120,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HSE Officer",
  "description": "SAVGRET Consulting Services is a dynamic firm specializing in providing tailored solutions to businesses and organizations across various industries. We strive to deliver exceptional consulting services with a focus on quality, integrity, and client satisfaction. Our mission is to help clients achieve operational excellence through innovative strategies and robust methods. At SAVGRET Consulting Services, we prioritize the professional development of our team members and offer a collaborative, growth-oriented environment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Officer
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

Role Description


	This is a full-time, on-site role based in Lagos for a Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Officer in the Construction Industry.
	The HSE Officer will be responsible for conducting risk assessments, ensuring compliance with safety regulations, and implementing workplace safety measures.
	Daily tasks include guiding construction teams on safe practices, conducting training sessions, inspecting sites for health and safety compliance, and preparing reports to address any occupational health and safety concerns.


Qualifications


	Experience in the construction industry or similar high-risk environments is preferred
	Relevant certification(s) in Health, Safety, and Environment or similar field
	Proficiency in Health &amp; Safety practices and Workplace Safety standards
	Thorough knowledge of Environment Health and Safety (EHS) regulations and policies
	Expertise in Occupational Health and NEBOSH certification is an advantage
	Strong communication skills for effective training and reporting
	Ability to analyze risks, develop preventive measures, and handle safety management systems.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;(resume) to:&nbsp;savgretconsulting@gmail.com&nbsp;/&nbsp;hrbp2@rhemaprojectng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

HSE Officer Job at Savgret Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        SAVGRET Consulting Services is a dynamic firm specializing in providing tailored solutions to businesses and organizations across various industries. We strive to deliver exceptional consulting services with a focus on quality, integrity, and client satisfaction. Our mission is to help clients achieve operational excellence through innovative strategies and robust methods. At SAVGRET Consulting Services, we prioritize the professional development of our team members and offer a collaborative, growth-oriented environment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Officer
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

Role Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This is a full-time, on-site role based in Lagos for a Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Officer in the Construction Industry.
	The HSE Officer will be responsible for conducting risk assessments, ensuring compliance with safety regulations, and implementing workplace safety measures.
	Daily tasks include guiding construction teams on safe practices, conducting training sessions, inspecting sites for health and safety compliance, and preparing reports to address any occupational health and safety concerns.


Qualifications


	Experience in the construction industry or similar high-risk environments is preferred
	Relevant certification(s) in Health, Safety, and Environment or similar field
	Proficiency in Health & Safety practices and Workplace Safety standards
	Thorough knowledge of Environment Health and Safety (EHS) regulations and policies
	Expertise in Occupational Health and NEBOSH certification is an advantage
	Strong communication skills for effective training and reporting
	Ability to analyze risks, develop preventive measures, and handle safety management systems.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV (resume) to: [email protected] / [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Receptionist",
  "description": "Eudora &amp; Aegle Management Consulting -&nbsp;We are a forward-thinking, impact-driven organization made up of three strategic subsidiaries that collectively exist to empower people, strengthen institutions, and shape the future of business and society.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Receptionist
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview


	This is an on-site and full-time role for Reception in Lagos.
	We are seeking a professional and people-oriented individual who will serve as the first point of contact for clients and customers, providing support with queries, documentation, and general administration.


Key&nbsp;Responsibilities


	Handle queries, calls, and correspondence
	Maintain organized administrative processes and the reception area
	Keep updated records of office expenses and costs
	Perform other receptionist duties as assigned


Requirements


	OND, HND, and BSc qualifications are required
	Proven work experience as a receptionist, front office representative, or similar role
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Close attention to detail and professionalism
	Basic computer literacy (MS Office)
	Professional attitude and appearance.


Salary
N70,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letteer to:&nbsp;hr@eudoraandaeglegroup.com&nbsp;and copy&nbsp;f.nwokoma@eudoraandaeglegroup.com&nbsp;using &quot;Receptionist&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Receptionist Job at Eudora & Aegle Management Consulting




        
    

        
        Eudora & Aegle Management Consulting - We are a forward-thinking, impact-driven organization made up of three strategic subsidiaries that collectively exist to empower people, strengthen institutions, and shape the future of business and society.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Receptionist
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This is an on-site and full-time role for Reception in Lagos.
	We are seeking a professional and people-oriented individual who will serve as the first point of contact for clients and customers, providing support with queries, documentation, and general administration.


Key Responsibilities


	Handle queries, calls, and correspondence
	Maintain organized administrative processes and the reception area
	Keep updated records of office expenses and costs
	Perform other receptionist duties as assigned


Requirements


	OND, HND, and BSc qualifications are required
	Proven work experience as a receptionist, front office representative, or similar role
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Close attention to detail and professionalism
	Basic computer literacy (MS Office)
	Professional attitude and appearance.


Salary
N70,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letteer to: [email protected] and copy [email protected] using "Receptionist" as the subject of the email.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HSE and Quality Control Manager",
  "description": "Oilserv Limited is a leading provider of integrated engineering, procurement, construction, installation &amp; commissioning (EPCIC) services and complementary solutions across the asset and program life cycle within the onshore, offshore and subsea market segments of the oil and gas sector. Oilserv Limited was incorporated in 1992 and commenced operations in 1995.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;HSE and Quality Control Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Umungasi, Abia&nbsp;
Job type: Full time

Job Summary


	Responsible for developing, implementing, and continuously improving HSE and quality management systems to ensure safe, compliant, and efficient plant operations.
	The role drives a strong safety culture, ensures adherence to regulatory and international standards, and safeguards personnel, assets, and the environment.


Job Details


	Develop and implement HSE and quality management systems; conduct risk assessments (HAZID/HAZOP) and safety audits; ensure compliance with regulatory, statutory, and company standards; oversee QA/QC processes across operations and maintenance; monitor HSE performance metrics and reporting; support commissioning and operational readiness from an HSE standpoint
	Lead incident investigations and root cause analysis; drive continuous improvement in safety performance; enforce safe work practices and permit-to-work systems; ensure emergency response preparedne


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Engineering, Environmental Sciences, or related field; NEBOSH, IOSH, or equivalent HSE certification (mandatory); additional quality certifications (ISO 9001/14001/45001) are an advantage
	7&ndash;10 years of experience in HSE within oil and gas, with exposure to gas processing, CNG, or similar facilities; experience in commissioning/startup phases is an added advantage.&nbsp;


Benefits


	Full HMO Cover
	Pension.

HSE and Quality Control Manager Job at Oilserv Limited




        
    

        
        Oilserv Limited is a leading provider of integrated engineering, procurement, construction, installation & commissioning (EPCIC) services and complementary solutions across the asset and program life cycle within the onshore, offshore and subsea market segments of the oil and gas sector. Oilserv Limited was incorporated in 1992 and commenced operations in 1995.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE and Quality Control Manager

Job Location: Umungasi, Abia 
Job type: Full time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Responsible for developing, implementing, and continuously improving HSE and quality management systems to ensure safe, compliant, and efficient plant operations.
	The role drives a strong safety culture, ensures adherence to regulatory and international standards, and safeguards personnel, assets, and the environment.


Job Details


	Develop and implement HSE and quality management systems; conduct risk assessments (HAZID/HAZOP) and safety audits; ensure compliance with regulatory, statutory, and company standards; oversee QA/QC processes across operations and maintenance; monitor HSE performance metrics and reporting; support commissioning and operational readiness from an HSE standpoint
	Lead incident investigations and root cause analysis; drive continuous improvement in safety performance; enforce safe work practices and permit-to-work systems; ensure emergency response preparedne


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Environmental Sciences, or related field; NEBOSH, IOSH, or equivalent HSE certification (mandatory); additional quality certifications (ISO 9001/14001/45001) are an advantage
	7–10 years of experience in HSE within oil and gas, with exposure to gas processing, CNG, or similar facilities; experience in commissioning/startup phases is an added advantage. 


Benefits


	Full HMO Cover
	Pension.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
